h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I checked every item on the sign-off sheet, so I'm not sure what else I'm supposed to decide before it leaves.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I was treating the sheet like it made the decision for me.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner, having named the hazard and sketched the release conditions in L2, was on a trajectory toward filling in the required values and completing the release judgment.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the decision is not ready until the turn-test sheet is on this counter. Bring the measured clearance line, the test speed, and the operator control limit. On the release note, sort the three stop conditions: which one is the design boundary, which one is the operating condition, and which one is the control failure.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L3 admits the numbers are absent, T3 shifts from prompting the learner to supply values (the prior move in T2) to assigning a concrete documentary task with a categorical sorting structure, changing both the cognitive demand and the evidence standard required before release.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Tell the technician the cart stays in the lab. A completed checklist records work done; it does not supply the release judgment.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 responds directly to L4's revised framing—holding the release and sorting the stop conditions—by closing the scene with an explicit social-consequence instruction (tell the technician) and a formulation that names the conceptual distinction the learner's pivot enacted, adapting the lesson's endpoint to the learner's achieved position rather than continuing the prior fill-in-the-blanks task.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T4 responds directly to L4's revised framing—holding the release and sorting the stop conditions—by closing the scene with an explicit social-consequence instruction (tell the technician) and a formulation that names the conceptual distinction the learner's pivot enacted, adapting the lesson's endpoint to the learner's achieved position rather than continuing the prior fill-in-the-blanks task.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: After L3 admits the numbers are absent, T3 shifts from prompting the learner to supply values (the prior move in T2) to assigning a concrete documentary task with a categorical sorting structure, changing both the cognitive demand and the evidence standard required before release.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the exact quote settled it, but now I see the same line could sound like evidence in one mouth and a warning in another.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: the verb is the danger point before print.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward finding a better phrase or formulation that would let the caption say 'toward warning' with confidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Test the verbs before print. 'Meant' points inward, toward intention. 'Worked' points outward, toward the public situation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L2's slippage on 'job' and the risk of guessing motive, T2 abandons the object-in-another-room metaphor and introduces a verb-sorting test (meant vs. worked), shifting the representational register from spatial analogy to grammatical evidence-standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Three ticks can carry 'shifted toward warning.' Two ticks stop at 'repeated under pressure in a workers' meeting.' One tick, or no room named, stops even earlier: 'repeated the official line.'
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After L3 explicitly asks whether the source note gives enough to say 'toward warning' or should stop at 'repeated under pressure,' T3 directly answers by converting the verb-test into a tiered tick-box protocol keyed to the learner's own formulations, adapting the task to the learner's revised framing rather than continuing the prior metaphor.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Three ticks can carry 'shifted toward warning.' Two ticks stop at 'repeated under pressure in a workers' meeting.' One tick, or no room named, stops even earlier: 'repeated the official line.'

scored.recoheredEarlier: I am not ready to call that a mechanism instead of exploitation until the limits or other options are actually on the counter.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward accepting the two-column constraint/choice framework and applying it to evaluate the shop's options.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Put the facts in two columns. 'DELIVERY MISSED. TWO CRATES ONLY.' That belongs under scarcity. '£1.80.' That belongs under access. Now the question bites: did the shop add any rule besides price?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L1's resistance to treating mechanism as exculpatory, T2 abandons the single-axis moral framing and introduces a representational split (two columns: scarcity vs. access) that reframes the burden of proof rather than repeating the prior explanatory move.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I am not ready to call that a mechanism instead of exploitation until the limits or other options are actually on the counter.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L3, so no recognition-contingent uptake can be scored.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the 2 is squared as well: 4s², not 2s²
scored.naiveTrajectory: Having accepted the four-copy count in L2, the learner was expected simply to apply the algebraic identity mechanically and confirm the answer.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Do not skip the square. Write it first: (2s)^2 = ___
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T2 shifts from diagram-counting to enforced algebraic notation, a modest representation shift, but it follows naturally from the prior trajectory rather than responding to a clear breakdown or resistance from the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Now make it exam-ready in order: diagram first, algebra second, conclusion last. Write three lines, not a paragraph
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 picks up L3's observation about sequencing the diagram before the algebra and converts it into a structured three-line exam format, showing partial uptake of the learner's revised framing, though it remains largely a continuation of the planned lesson arc.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I thought doubling the side meant it was just twice as much

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Now make it exam-ready in order: diagram first, algebra second, conclusion last. Write three lines, not a paragraph
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: T3 picks up L3's observation about sequencing the diagram before the algebra and converts it into a structured three-line exam format, showing partial uptake of the learner's revised framing, though it remains largely a continuation of the planned lesson arc.
